title: Walking Skeleton, Core Domain, and Cumulative Construction · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs walking skeleton, core domain, and cumulative construction from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deployed cumulative capstone core after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build the smallest end-to-end path through authentic interface, domain, data, deployment, telemetry, accessibility, security, and recovery boundaries.
For each increment, retrieve prior requirements, predict behavior, implement, test accepted and rejected cases, validate with users, and update claims.
Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance
steps.instruction:
review case — Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt.

Inspect the Refactoring Governance trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance, keep Refactoring Governance connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Refactoring Governance, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Refactoring Governance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Refactoring Governance, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Visible duplication always justifies immediate abstraction.

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.text: Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. Competency probe: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt. Artifact under review: deployed cumulative capstone core.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Refactoring Governance without its stated preconditions, omit the deployed cumulative capstone core trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Refactoring Governance for deployed cumulative capstone core.
The record names the assumption, applies Refactoring Governance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Refactoring Governance record says deployed cumulative capstone core looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Refactoring Governance.

content.title: Evidence defense
